Package i5.cb.graph.diagram

Class DiagramNode

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • javax.swing.JInternalFrame
            • i5.cb.graph.diagram.DiagramNode
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    DiagramObject, ILangChangeable

    public class DiagramNode
extends javax.swing.JInternalFrame
implements DiagramObject, ILangChangeable
    A DiagramNode is a visible node shown on a DiagramDesktop to represent a certain userobject. Two DiagramNodes may be connected via a DiagramEdge. A DiagramNode may also 'sit' on a DiagramEdge.q
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void addEdge​(DiagramEdge de)
      Is called by the constructor of DiagramEdge to add itself to the edges coming out or going into this node.
      void addPropertyChangeListener​(java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
      Adds a PropertyChangeListener to the listener list.
      boolean containsNode​(DiagramNode dn)
      checks whether the center point of dn is contained in this DiagramNode
      void designateLocation​(java.awt.Point p)
      Sets this diagram node's designated location to p; the designation is typically set by the graph property "location" of this node.
      void drawInvalidSign​(java.awt.Graphics g)
      Kind of crosses out this node on its diagramDesktop which should be the only place to call this method from.
      void drawRect​(java.awt.Graphics g)
      Draws a red rectangle around this node to show that it's marked.
      void erase​()
      Removes this node from its diagramDesktop.
      java.awt.Point getCenter​()
      Gets the center of this node's bounding-rectangel
      java.awt.Point getDesignatedLocation​()  
      DiagramClass getDiagramClass​()
      Gets the DiagramClass which defines this DiagramNode's properties such as it's popupmenu.
      DiagramDesktop getDiagramDesktop​()
      Gets the DiagramDesktop this node is shown on.
      DiagramEdge getDiagramEdge​()
      Gets the DiagramEdge this DiagramNode is on or null if this node is not on a DiagramEdge.
      java.util.Vector getEdges​()
      getEdges returns the edges connected to this node.
      javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument getInfoDoc​()
      Gets this node's infoDocument by calling the method of this node's DiagramClass.
      java.lang.String getLabel​()  
      DiagramNode getNode​()
      Returns this node itself
      int getNodeLevel​()
      Getter for property m_nodeLevel.
      java.awt.Shape getShape​()
      Return the Shape of this node.
      java.lang.Object getUserObject​()
      Gets the userObject this node represents on the DiagramDesktop.
      java.awt.Component getVisibleComponent​()
      Returns the component which is currently visible (i.e.
      boolean hasFixedPosition​()
      Determines if this DiagramObject has a fixed position on the DiagramDesktop.
      boolean hasSquareDot​()  
      boolean isComponentVisible​()
      Tells if the Component this node is associated with is visible.
      boolean isDragged​()
      Getter for property isDragged.
      boolean isFrozen​()
      Getter for property isFrozen.
      boolean isOnEdge​()
      Tells if this DiagramNode is on a DiagramEdge
      boolean isShown​()
      tells if this node has been added to the diagramDesktop.
      boolean isSibling​(DiagramObject diagObj)
      Tells if this node is a sibling of another node.
      boolean isSmallComponentVisible​()
      Tells if the Component this node is associated with is visible.
      boolean isValid​()
      Getter for property valid.

      This node's paint method.

      Paints the Shape associated with this DiagramNode.

      Tells this DiagramNode that it is not the source or the destination of a particular DiagramEdge anymore.

      Removes a PropertyChangeListener from the listener list.
      void resizeComponents​()
      Take care that the rootpane, glasspane, layered pan and content pane of this DiagramNode have the right sizes.

      Sets this node's location in a way that its bounding-rectangle's center ist at newCenter and notifies the DiagramDesktop

    • Constructor Detail

      • DiagramNode

        public DiagramNode​(java.lang.Object uo,
                   DiagramClass dc)
        Creates a new DiagramNode instance. This constructor is used if an ordinary node is desired, which does not sit on a DiagramEdge.
        Parameters:
        uo - an Object value. The userObject this node is to represent
        dc - a DiagramClass value. The DiagramClass that defines this node's properties

    • Method Detail

      • getUserObject

        public final java.lang.Object getUserObject​()
        Gets the userObject this node represents on the DiagramDesktop.
        Specified by:
        getUserObject in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        an Object value. This DiagramNode's userobject.

      • setUserObject

        public void setUserObject​(java.lang.Object uo)
        Sets the Diagram Node's user object to uo. Required for updating the view of of a diagram object
        Parameters:
        uo - the diagram node's user object to be set to
        See Also:
        DiagramObject

      • getLabel

        public java.lang.String getLabel​()

      • setSmallComponentSize

        public void setSmallComponentSize​(java.awt.Dimension dim)
        Sets this diagram nodes small component size to dim if dim is not null
        Parameters:
        dim - the proposed new small component size

      • designateLocation

        public void designateLocation​(java.awt.Point p)
        Sets this diagram node's designated location to p; the designation is typically set by the graph property "location" of this node.
        Parameters:
        p - the designated location of this diagram node

      • getDesignatedLocation

        public java.awt.Point getDesignatedLocation​()

      • setPaintShapePolicy

        public final void setPaintShapePolicy​(int policy)
        Tells this node whether it shall draw it's shape. Possible values are PAINT_SHAPE_ALWAYS, PAINT_SHAPE_COMPONENT, PAINT_SHAPE_SMALLCOMPONET and PAINT_SHAPE_NEVER(default)

      • getDiagramDesktop

        public final DiagramDesktop getDiagramDesktop​()
        Gets the DiagramDesktop this node is shown on.
        Specified by:
        getDiagramDesktop in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a DiagramDesktop value

        public final void setDiagramDesktop​(DiagramDesktop dd)

        public final DiagramClass getDiagramClass​()

        Specified by:
        getDiagramClass in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a DiagramClass value

      • addEdge

        public final void addEdge​(DiagramEdge de)
        Is called by the constructor of DiagramEdge to add itself to the edges coming out or going into this node. If this node is on an edge, the edge is not added to this node's edges.
        Specified by:
        addEdge in interface DiagramObject
        Parameters:
        de - the diagramEdge to be added

      • removeEdge

        public final boolean removeEdge​(DiagramEdge e)
        Tells this DiagramNode that it is not the source or the destination of a particular DiagramEdge anymore.
        Specified by:
        removeEdge in interface DiagramObject
        Parameters:
        e - a DiagramEdge value. The DiagramEdge that shall be removed from the collection of this DiagramNode's edges.
        Returns:
        true if the edge was connected to this node.

      • getEdges

        public final java.util.Vector getEdges​()
        getEdges returns the edges connected to this node.
        Specified by:
        getEdges in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a Vector containing all edges linked to this node

      • erase

        public final void erase​()
        Removes this node from its diagramDesktop. Also erases all DiagramEdges that were connected to this node.
        Specified by:
        erase in interface DiagramObject

      • isComponentVisible

        public final boolean isComponentVisible​()
        Tells if the Component this node is associated with is visible. The association is managed by DiagramClass
        Specified by:
        isComponentVisible in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        True if this node's Component is currently visible

      • isSmallComponentVisible

        public final boolean isSmallComponentVisible​()
        Tells if the Component this node is associated with is visible. The association is managed by DiagramClass
        Specified by:
        isSmallComponentVisible in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        True if this node's SmallComponent is currently visible

      • toggleComponentView

        public void toggleComponentView​()
        Description of the Method

      • setComponentVisible

        public final boolean setComponentVisible​()
        Description copied from interface: DiagramObject
        Set this DiagramObject's Component visible. Only one of the components "component" and smallComponent" can be visible at the same time. So setting the one visible means to set the other one visible.
        Specified by:
        setComponentVisible in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • setComponentVisible

        public final boolean setComponentVisible​(boolean forceSet)
        Sets this DiagramNode's "Component" visible together with a small empty titlebar. Every DiagramNode is associated with two Components. One of them is referred to as "Component " and one as "SmallComponent". They cannot be shown at the same time. The titlebar can be covered by the DiagramGlassPane, so that you can move the node and still have full access to the complete Component.
        Parameters:
        forceSet - true if the seeting shall be enforced
        Returns:
        true if the node's "Component" was already visible or if it was successfully set visible. False if there was no "Component" associated with this node.

      • setSmallComponentVisible

        public final boolean setSmallComponentVisible​()
        Description copied from interface: DiagramObject
        Set this DiagramObject's smallComponent visible. Only one of the components "component" and smallComponent" can be visible at the same time. So setting the one visible means to set the other one visible.
        Specified by:
        setSmallComponentVisible in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • setSmallComponentVisible

        public final boolean setSmallComponentVisible​(boolean forceSet)
        Sets this DiagramNode's "SmallComponent" visible. Every DiagramNode is associated with two Components. One of them is referred to as "Component " and one as "SmallComponent". They cannot be shown at the same time. The actuall Components are below the properties managed by DiagramClass
        Parameters:
        forceSet - true if the seeting shall be enforced
        Returns:
        true if the node's "SmallComponent" was already visible or if it was successfully set visible. False if there was no "SmallComponent" associated with this node.

      • resizeComponents

        public final void resizeComponents​()
        Take care that the rootpane, glasspane, layered pan and content pane of this DiagramNode have the right sizes. That was done automatically by Swing up to Java 6 Update 11.

      • getInfoDoc

        public final javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument getInfoDoc​()
        Gets this node's infoDocument by calling the method of this node's DiagramClass. This document contains information about the userobjet this node represents and is shown in the grapheditor's informationwindow.
        Returns:
        a DefaultStyledDocument value

      • isShown

        public final boolean isShown​()
        tells if this node has been added to the diagramDesktop. adding a node to the dd means that it has been made visible
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • containsNode

        public boolean containsNode​(DiagramNode dn)
        checks whether the center point of dn is contained in this DiagramNode
        Returns:
        true if tn and this DiagramNode are different proper nodes and the center of dn is contained in this DiagramNode's bounds

      • paint

        public final void paint​(java.awt.Graphics g)
        This node's paint method.
        Specified by:
        paint in interface DiagramObject
        Overrides:
        paint in class javax.swing.JComponent
        Parameters:
        g - a Graphics value

      • drawRect

        public final void drawRect​(java.awt.Graphics g)
        Draws a red rectangle around this node to show that it's marked. Called by its diagramDesktop which should be the only place to call this method from.
        Parameters:
        g -

      • drawInvalidSign

        public final void drawInvalidSign​(java.awt.Graphics g)
        Kind of crosses out this node on its diagramDesktop which should be the only place to call this method from.
        Parameters:
        g -

      • getShape

        public final java.awt.Shape getShape​()
        Return the Shape of this node. This includes the area of the JInternalFrame and the area of the shape if it is visible and not null. Note, that this method returns the shape of the whole diagram node and not only the shape that is painted when the diagram node shows the small component.
        Specified by:
        getShape in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        the shape of this DiagramNode.

      • getCenter

        public final java.awt.Point getCenter​()
        Gets the center of this node's bounding-rectangel

      • setCenter

        public final void setCenter​(java.awt.Point newCenter)
        Sets this node's location in a way that its bounding-rectangle's center ist at newCenter and notifies the DiagramDesktop

                            int y)

        Tells if this DiagramNode is on a DiagramEdge
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • hasFixedPosition

        public boolean hasFixedPosition​()
        Description copied from interface: DiagramObject
        Determines if this DiagramObject has a fixed position on the DiagramDesktop. This member's value is true for DiagramEdges for example as they are not to be removed directly but by removing their source- and destinationnode and the node sitting on it. I really like long sentences.
        Specified by:
        hasFixedPosition in interface DiagramObject
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • paintShape

        protected void paintShape​(java.awt.Graphics g)
        Paints the Shape associated with this DiagramNode. The association is managed by this node's DiagramClass
        Parameters:
        g - a Graphics value

      • paintShape

        protected void paintShape​(java.awt.Graphics g1,
                          boolean flagFillShape)

      • updateLang

        public javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument updateLang​(java.util.Locale l)
        Description copied from interface: ILangChangeable
        Updates the language in which labels and other information are written
        Specified by:
        updateLang in interface ILangChangeable
        Returns:
        an DefaultStyledDocument. This can be null, for example if this object has just some labels or menuentries needed to be updated or another translation and/or style of the information this object provides

      • setBundle

        public final void setBundle​(java.util.ResourceBundle bundle)
        Setter for property bundle.
        Parameters:
        bundle - New value of property bundle.

      • addPropertyChangeListener

        public final void addPropertyChangeListener​(java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
        Adds a PropertyChangeListener to the listener list.
        Overrides:
        addPropertyChangeListener in class java.awt.Container
        Parameters:
        l - The listener to add.

      • removePropertyChangeListener

        public final void removePropertyChangeListener​(java.beans.PropertyChangeListener l)
        Removes a PropertyChangeListener from the listener list.
        Overrides:
        removePropertyChangeListener in class java.awt.Component
        Parameters:
        l - The listener to remove.

      • getDiagramEdge

        public final DiagramEdge getDiagramEdge​()
        Gets the DiagramEdge this DiagramNode is on or null if this node is not on a DiagramEdge.
        Returns:
        a DiagramEdge value

      • setDiagramEdge

        public final void setDiagramEdge​(DiagramEdge diagramEdge)
        Setter for property diagramEdge. diagramEdge may not be null.
        Parameters:
        diagramEdge - New value of property diagramEdge. If the param is null, this node is added to the diagramDesktop's edgelayer

      • getNode

        public final DiagramNode getNode​()
        Returns this node itself
        Specified by:
        getNode in interface DiagramObject

      • isSibling

        public final boolean isSibling​(DiagramObject diagObj)
        Tells if this node is a sibling of another node.
        Returns:
        true iff the two nodes are on two edges that are incident with the same node or if the two nodes are adjacent to the same node

      • getVisibleComponent

        public final java.awt.Component getVisibleComponent​()
        Returns the component which is currently visible (i.e. small component or *the* component)

      • isValid

        public final boolean isValid​()
        Getter for property valid.
        Overrides:
        isValid in class java.awt.Component
        Returns:
        true iff this node (or its userobject) is still valid in a certain sense, which is usually related to the userObject

      • setValid

        public final void setValid​(boolean valid)
        Setter for property valid.
        Parameters:
        valid - New value of property valid.

      • updateUI

        public void updateUI​()
        Overrides:
        updateUI in class javax.swing.JInternalFrame

      • isDragged

        public boolean isDragged​()
        Getter for property isDragged.
        Returns:
        true iff this node is currently being dragged (see DiagramNodeMouseListener.java)

      • setDragged

        public void setDragged​(boolean newDragged)
        Setter for property isDragged.
        Parameters:
        newDragged - New value of property isDragged.

      • isFrozen

        public boolean isFrozen​()
        Getter for property isFrozen.
        Returns:
        true iff this node is currently being frozen (see DiagramNodeMouseListener.java)

      • setFrozen

        public void setFrozen​(boolean newFrozen)
        Setter for property isFrozen.
        Parameters:
        newFrozen - New value of property isFrozen.

      • getNodeLevel

        public int getNodeLevel​()
        Getter for property m_nodeLevel.
        Returns:
        the nodelevel of this node relative to DiagramDesktop.NODE_LAYER (=JLayeredPane.MODAL_LAYER=Integer(200))

      • setNodeLevel

        public void setNodeLevel​(java.lang.String s)

      • setNodeLevel

        public void setNodeLevel​(int nodelevel)

      • setSquareDot

        public void setSquareDot​(boolean visible)
        set the square dot of the diagram labels of this diagram node with empty text visible via its opacity; only called for diagram nodes that sit on edges; called in DiagramDesktop.setSquareDots

      • hasSquareDot

        public boolean hasSquareDot​()
